title: 🦞 How to set up OpenClaw across WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, Slack
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# How to set up OpenClaw across WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord and Slack. How to set up OpenClaw across WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord and Slack. Multi-channel is the feature that makes OpenClaw feel “everywhere” without turning into a mess of separate bots. Multi-channel does not mean “I installed three bots.” It means one long-running Gateway process receives messages from different platforms and routes them into the same session store. The official OpenClaw docs describe the Gateway as the central control plane for channels, sessions, and tool execution. WhatsApp is the channel most people want first because it is already where they live. Discord is where multi-channel starts to feel like a team tool. Unlike WhatsApp or Telegram, Discord is structured around servers, channels, threads, and roles. If you want OpenClaw to be a shared assistant for a team, Discord is often a better fit than WhatsApp. If you run multi-channel OpenClaw, assume someone will try to poke it.

title: 🦞 How to Use Cron Jobs in OpenClaw to Automate Nightly Tasks Without ...
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

Scheduling OpenClaw crons overnight preserves your rolling quota window for daytime use. The fix isn’t a bigger plan — it’s scheduling cron jobs to run overnight, when your rolling quota window is clear. This guide walks through how to set up nightly cron jobs in OpenClaw, schedule them intelligently, and build in the error handling that overnight automation requires. Cron jobs are scheduled tasks — automations that run at times you define, without any manual trigger. Your heaviest jobs — the ones consuming the most requests — should run early in the night window (1–2 AM). Teams already using OpenClaw sometimes use MindStudio as an orchestration layer: OpenClaw handles specific task execution while a MindStudio background agent manages scheduling, sequencing between jobs, and error routing across the full overnight window. ### When is the best time to schedule OpenClaw cron jobs overnight? * **Overnight jobs need strong error handling.** Idempotent operations, exponential backoff, failure alerts, and job logs are baseline requirements — not optional extras.

# OpenClaw cron jobs: automate your AI agent while you sleep. * OpenClaw (previously known as Clawdbot and Moltbot) has built-in cron jobs that let your agent run tasks on a schedule without you being present. * What are OpenClaw cron jobs. ## What are OpenClaw cron jobs. Cron jobs are scheduled tasks that your OpenClaw agent executes automatically. An agent with cron jobs runs scheduled tasks at 7 AM, backs up files every hour, and sends weekly reports without manual triggering. Just tell your agent what you want scheduled and it will create the cron job for you. > "Create a cron job that runs every morning at 7 AM UTC. When a cron job runs in the main session, it has access to the full conversation history. For a complete walkthrough of building a morning briefing cron job, see OpenClaw Morning Briefing: 10-Minute Setup That Actually Works. Q: How many cron jobs can I run? * OpenClaw documentation on cron jobs.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide | MI - 超智諮詢
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide. Diagram of OpenClaw Cron scheduled task automation execution. If you're familiar with Linux's `crontab`, OpenClaw's Cron is conceptually similar -- it automatically executes tasks at specified times. But the key difference is: traditional crontab executes fixed Shell commands, while OpenClaw's Cron executes **natural language instructions**, with the AI agent autonomously deciding how to complete them. If a Cron task's execution time exceeds this value, the Gateway will forcibly terminate the connection, causing the task to be truncated -- the agent may have completed 80% of the work, but cannot return results due to the timeout. Cron is the key feature that transforms OpenClaw from a "passive tool" into a "proactive assistant." Once configured, the agent automatically executes tasks and reports results at your specified times -- you don't need to remember what to check, don't need to open a terminal, and don't need to be at the computer.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw Tip #2: Set Up Cron Jobs for Automated Daily Tasks
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw Tip #2: Set Up Cron Jobs for Automated Daily Tasks. OpenClaw cron job automation - futuristic clock mechanism with digital circuits. One of OpenClaw’s most powerful features is its built-in **cron job scheduler**. ## How OpenClaw Cron Jobs Work. Cron jobs run inside the Gateway process, not the AI model itself. Jobs are persisted at `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json`, so they survive restarts. ## Creating Your First Cron Job. ### One-Shot Reminder. --system-event "Reminder: review the weekly report" \. ## Useful Cron Job Ideas. ## Managing Cron Jobs. # Run a job manually. openclaw cron run <job-id>. openclaw cron runs --id <job-id>. # Delete a job. openclaw cron delete <job-id>`. **Pro tip:** Schedule resource-intensive cron jobs during off-peak hours (late night) to avoid hitting API rate limits during your active working hours. ##### Share this post. ## Related posts. OpenClaw Tip #18 featured image: tighten gateway file permissions. ##### Will AI Take Your Job in the Next 10 Years?

title: 🦞 cron - OpenClaw Docs
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# `openclaw cron`. Note: isolated `cron add` jobs default to `--announce` delivery. Note: `openclaw cron run` now returns as soon as the manual run is queued for execution. Upgrade note: if you have older cron jobs from before the current delivery/store format, run `openclaw doctor --fix`. openclaw cron edit <job-id> --announce --channel telegram --to "123456789" openclaw  cron  edit <job-i d> --announce --channel  telegram --to  "123456789". openclaw cron edit <job-id> --no-deliver openclaw  cron  edit <job-i d> --no-deliver. openclaw cron edit <job-id> --light-context openclaw  cron  edit <job-i d> --light-context. openclaw cron edit <job-id> --announce --channel slack --to "channel:C1234567890" openclaw  cron  edit <job-i d> --announce --channel  slack --to "channel:C1234567890". openclaw cron add \ openclaw  cron  add  \ --name "Lightweight morning brief" \ --name  "Lightweight morning brief"  \ --cron "0 7 * * *" \ --cron "0 7 * * *"  \ --session isolated \ --session  isolated  \ --message "Summarize overnight updates." \ --message "Summarize overnight updates."  \ --light-context \ --light-context  \ --no-deliver --no-deliver.
